text.title: Video Prompt Engineering — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: SASSCL formula, provider-quirk overlay, 5-gen eval gate before promotion, per-provider max prompt length, brand style locked.

rule.statement: Production prompts MUST include Subject + Action + Setting + Style + Camera + Lighting fields. Missing any field is forbidden in promoted templates.
rule.rationale: The 6 fields cover the variance dimensions of generated video. Missing any field produces drift on that dimension.

rule.statement: Provider-specific overlay MUST adjust the base SASSCL: Runway camera vocabulary (dolly, tracking, orbit), Luma prose-style sensory language, Sora multi-stage temporal, Veo audio descriptions + 4K.
rule.rationale: Each model has different training distribution. Same SASSCL prompt produces different quality across providers; per-provider tweaks close the gap.

rule.statement: Before promotion, a prompt template MUST be evaluated against 5 generations rated on the eval-rubric. Promotion threshold: ≥4/5 pass. Direct production deploy without eval forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single generation may pass on luck. 5-gen sample reveals variance; 4/5 threshold filters lucky outliers.

rule.statement: Prompt length MUST respect provider cap: Runway ≤500 chars, Luma ≤1500 chars, Veo ≤2000 chars, Sora ≤4000 chars. Overflow truncates silently and changes semantics.
rule.rationale: Providers truncate without warning. Verified length in template prevents silent truncation surprises.

rule.statement: The Style, Camera, Lighting fields MUST be locked per brand. Only Subject and Action vary between generations. Free-form Style across generations breaks brand consistency.
rule.rationale: A media brand needs visual coherence across all generated content. Locking 3 of 6 fields enforces it at the template level.
